questionText: API latency is variable & app is sluggish. How do you design UI safely?

whatWorkedWell: Correctly identified skeleton screens as the go-to pattern for managing perceived latency.
Proposed optimistic UI updates to provide instant feedback before API confirmation.
Demonstrated strong understanding of retry logic with exponential backoff.
Mentioned SWR (stale-while-revalidate) caching to reduce redundant network calls.
Clearly articulated how to degrade gracefully when network conditions worsen.
overallTakeaways: Explore offline-first architecture using local-first databases like WatermelonDB.
Deep dive into React Query or SWR for advanced cache invalidation strategies.
Practice explaining the trade-off between real-time accuracy and UI smoothness.
Review how apps like Zomato and Swiggy handle variable network conditions in India.
Consider adding timeout thresholds and user-facing error states to your answer.

keyMoments.description: Correctly explained skeleton screens to handle variable API latency effectively.
Clearly articulated optimistic UI updates for instant user feedback on slow networks.
Accurately described exponential backoff as fundamental to building resilient retry logic.
Struggled to explain cache invalidation strategies when underlying data updates frequently.
Recovered well by mentioning React Query's built-in background refetching mechanism.
Good mention of lazy loading and code splitting for non-critical UI components.
Could have been more specific about network timeout thresholds and error boundary placement.
